meaning: n.不安的状态,动荡的局面
examples: Unrest has spilt over into areas outside the city.
A period of unrest followed the president's resignation.
The unrest capped a weekend of right-wing attacks on foreigners.
The 57-year-old director added that despite rumours of unrest in the boardroom, he is there to stay.
At least 12, 000 Muslims are thought to have fled their homes in the unrest since then.
